Home › Volusia County

County hub · Updated June 2026

Volusia County Restaurant Health Grades

60%Grade A
4.3Avg rating
246Restaurants
7Cities

Explore 246 restaurants across Volusia County in Central Florida, each with its latest health inspection grade, dietary accommodations, and diner ratings. 148 currently hold an A health grade. Use the list below to compare scores and pick where to eat.

Cities in Volusia County

  • Daytona Beach 80
  • New Smyrna Beach 32
  • Ormond Beach 27
  • Deland 27
  • Orange City 23
  • Port Orange 21
  • Daytona Beach Shores 11

  • Lowest Health Scores in Volusia County (31)

Volusia County restaurant health grades — FAQ

How are Volusia County restaurant health grades calculated?

Florida issues no official grades, so we calculate an A–F score from each restaurant's most recent DBPR inspection — starting at 100 points and deducting for high-priority, intermediate, and basic violations. Of the 246 Volusia County restaurants we grade, 148 currently hold an A.

Does Florida assign letter grades to Volusia County restaurants?

No. The Florida Department of Business and Professional Regulation (DBPR) inspects restaurants and records violations but does not grade them. The grades shown here are calculated by us from those public inspection records.

How many restaurants are graded in Volusia County?

We currently publish 246 graded restaurants in Volusia County, with an average diner rating of 4.3 stars; 148 hold an A health grade.

See the full scoring method on our methodology page.

Central Florida Restaurants

Restaurant health inspection grades, dietary options, and diner ratings across Central Florida. Inspection data from Florida DBPR public records.
